summary:
Treatment as prevention through sustained HIV viral load suppression is clinically supported by multiple effective antiretroviral drug classes. With adequate access to combined therapy and established programs such as PrEP, maintaining viral suppression—even in low-resource areas—is both possible and feasible.  

Note: This summary focuses only on direct clinical relevance related to feasibility of achieving viral suppression for preventing transmission.
